title: Bento Box
description: A modular library for expressive GUIs and declarative configs
body:
# Bento Box

Bento Box is a modular library with expressive GUIs, declarative configs, and *more down the road!*

## For developers

Thanks to Bento Box being modular, you can depend on just the modules you need without pulling in everything else.

Bento modules include:

- [Bento Config](https://modrinth.com/mod/bento-config), a declarative configuration system built using Bento GUI. It aims to be simple yet powerful, with no magic pre-processors or arbitrary limitations.

- [Bento GUI](https://modrinth.com/mod/bento-gui), an expressive GUI library that makes building dynamic screens fast and easy. It is a replacement for Minecraft’s UI widgets that is inspired by HTML and CSS, and uses the builder pattern for building GUI elements.

- [Bento Util](https://modrinth.com/mod/bento-util), a general utility library that other Bento modules tend to make use of. It has a few things here you might find useful yourself!

Bento Box is documented in-code with JavaDocs and on [my wiki](https://docs.chailotl.dev/).

## For users

If you have multiple Bento modules (i.e. Bento Config, Bento GUI) in your mod list, you can replace them all with just Bento Box.
